scope: Deep-audit all project QoL automations, recent scheduled-run evidence, live prompt snapshots, logging requirements, and unattended script paths; repair any project-owned blocker found.
validation: Live registry inspection found 30 ACTIVE Senior Capstone QoL automations with single BYHOUR RRULEs and the expected 00:03 through 23:15 daily spread.
Prompt snapshots regenerated from the live TOMLs with scripts/snapshot-automation-prompts.ps1.
Strict live automation contract passed for 30 QoL automations and 2 support automations.
Efficiency scorecard wrote docs/audits/automation-qol-deep-audit-scorecard-2026-05-19.json and reported 30 active QoL automations, 30 starts/day, 900 starts/30 days, and 48-minute minimum spacing.
Codex session index telemetry showed 18 observed QoL scheduled sessions in the 30-day window; missing main-repo manifests are now treated as closeout failures rather than scheduler failures.
powershell -File scripts/run-npm-script.ps1 check:site-options passed for 3 site roots and their manifest pages.
powershell -File scripts/run-npm-script.ps1 test passed with 7 Node tests.
evidence: Logs showed earlier runs hit stale snapshots, missing admin-ops logs, missing memory files, WindowsApps node access denied, missing npm, and closeout gaps.
All 30 live QoL prompts now point unattended runs at scripts/run-node-script.ps1 and scripts/run-npm-script.ps1 before relying on npm/plain node.
The contract checker now fails automation prompts that reference undefined npm scripts.
The public-site support automation's referenced check:site-options command now exists and validates generated site roots without rewriting timestamps.

self_improvement.evidence: Bryan requested that every QoL automation be able to run on its own; live prompt and script repairs now remove the repeated node/npm and undefined-script blockers.

automation_efficiency.notes: No cadence reduction was made. The healthy 30-start/day schedule remains active; the repair focuses on run closeout, wrapper reliability, prompt drift detection, and package-script contract enforcement.

next: Future QoL runs should land one structured repo manifest plus one phone-tracker row, or record the exact blocker if the Google Sheets append or commit/push is unavailable.
If a scheduled session fires but no main-repo manifest appears, classify it as closeout failure evidence for the next script/prompt audit.
